metadata.source_note: Official HMRC guidance page publishing National Insurance contribution rates and allowances per tax year, including the Class 3 voluntary flat weekly rate (2026 to 2027: GBP 18.40; 2025 to 2026: GBP 17.75). Annual re-ratings are made by administrative order and are not consolidated into the SSCBA 1992 s.13(1) base text on legislation.gov.uk (which still reads GBP 17.75), so this page carries the money atoms for Class 3 weekly amounts from 6 April 2026. Page last updated 6 April 2026.
metadata.title: Rates and allowances: National Insurance contributions

body:
HM Revenue & Customs

© Crown copyright 2026

This publication is licensed under the terms of the Open Government Licence v3.0 except where otherwise stated. To view this licence, visit nationalarchives.gov.uk/doc/open-government-licence/version/3 or write to the Information Policy Team, The National Archives, Kew, London TW9 4DU, or email: psi@nationalarchives.gov.uk .

Where we have identified any third party copyright information you will need to obtain permission from the copyright holders concerned.

This publication is available at https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/rates-and-allowances-national-insurance-contributions/rates-and-allowances-national-insurance-contributions

There are different ‘classes’ of National Insurance (NI) . The type people pay depends on their employment status and how much they earn.

New National Insurance bands and allowances are usually announced in the Chancellor of the Exchequer’s Budget or Autumn Statement.

body: Employers and employees pay Class 1 National Insurance depending on how much the employee earns. You can view these earnings thresholds by week (table 1.1) or by month (table 1.2).

body:
This table shows how much employers pay towards their employees’ National Insurance.

Percentage rate 2026 to 2027 2025 to 2026 2024 to 2025 2023 to 2024 From 6 November 2022 to 5 April 2023 Rate above the Secondary Threshold 15% 15% 13.8% 13.8% 13.8% Rate below Upper Secondary Threshold 0% 0% 0% 0% 0% Rate below Freeports Upper Secondary Threshold 0% 0% 0% 0% 0% Rate below Apprentice Upper Secondary Threshold 0% 0% 0% 0% 0% Rate below Investment Zones Upper Secondary Threshold 0% 0% 0% Not applicable Not applicable Rate below Veterans Upper Secondary Threshold 0% 0% 0% 0% 0% Class 1A rate on expenses and benefits 15% 15% 13.8% 13.8% 14.53%

The rate shown in the fourth column of the table (from 6 November 2022) is the Class 1A rate that applies to the whole tax year because Class 1A is an annualised charge. Read CWG5 for the rates that apply to termination awards and sporting testimonials paid through real time information .

For 2023 to 2024 and 2022 to 2023, the National Insurance contributions rates for directors are different. Read CA44 to find out more about rates for directors .

metadata.title: Rates and allowances: National Insurance contributions

body:
You can pay voluntary National Insurance to fill or avoid gaps in your record.

If you’re paying voluntary contributions for the previous 2 tax years (2023 to 2024 or 2022 to 2023), you’ll pay the original rates for those years.

For all other years you’ll pay the current rate (2024 to 2025).

£ per week 2026 to 2027 2025 to 2026 2024 to 2025 2023 to 2024 Class 3 rate £18.40 £17.75 £17.45 £17.45
